comment.body:
Thank you, @Alma, and thanks to everyone here.
Wishing you all a peaceful, healthy and wise 2026.
I'm grateful for all the good I received in 2025.
My lesson for 2025 was, adapting from Forrest Gump, "the market is as the market does".
My trading behavior improvement goals for 2026 are, in order or priority:
1. Be open and adaptable; see things as they are, not as I would like them to be
2. Follow my rules
3. Don't size too big
4. Know when to exit (kelly in; kelly out)
5. Don't size too small

comment.body:
My sense is that everything is soaked in yin, and nothing is priced.
The era of yang paying off has long since passed ("no new continents to colonize").
Yin is like a semi-dead crypto market where participants are mostly mean-rev bots and market-maker bots; the energy of position/risk takers gets dissipated and is not rewarded.
At a wider level, in this environment, it seems best not to over-stretch.
But, there has been so much yin and sapping of market energy, that nothing appears to be priced correctly (bonds, FX, stock indices).
While excessive yang (position/risk taking) will not be rewarded, excessive yin (betting on "nothing happening") will not be rewarded either. I think that we have reached the peak of “betting on nothing happening” paying off.
In 2026, within a multi-year context of energy dissipation, the "stop the dip"/"urgency is sold" behavior may well recede, bit by bit, to something more balanced.
Anyhow, wishing you all insightful and timely trading and not trading in 2026.
